title: Visual pose editor — 3-D three.js mannequin + 2-D drawn figure + photo frames
reason: Dormant since the Stickman/Photo/3D mode UIs that drove it were removed: nothing emits a .pose3d/.pose-draw/.pose-photo-scrub container, so the figures could never mount. But it kept pulling ~1 MB of three.js (poseScene) + 170 KB of base64 frames (poseFrames) onto EVERY page's download + parse path, hurting load smoothness for code that could not run. Warehoused (not deleted) because it is parked for the 'visual variation picker' rebuild.

sharedState: (none — figures owned their own activePose3d/activeDrawn handles, also removed)

wiredInto: src/main.ts refreshPoseViz() — was refreshPose3d()+refreshDrawn(); now a no-op stub kept for its (dormant) callers
src/main.ts .pose-ctl click handler — removed the 2 live activePose3d/activeDrawn .update(vec) lines
src/main.ts onScrub (.pose-scrub input) — removed the activeDrawn .update/.scrub line + unused frac
src/main.ts .pose-scrub change handler — removed the activeDrawn.fig.scrub(null) line
src/main.ts .pose-photo-scrub input handler — deleted entirely (was the sole POSE_FRAMES reader)
restoreSteps: npm install three @types/three  (re-add to package.json dependencies).
Copy poseScene.ts, poseDraw.ts, poseFrames.ts back into src/.
In src/main.ts re-add the imports: mountPoseScene/PoseScene, mountPoseDraw/PoseDraw, POSE_FRAMES.
Restore the activePose3d/activeDrawn state + refreshPose3d()/refreshDrawn() and make refreshPoseViz() call them again (see git history of the removal commit for the verbatim bodies).
Restore the .pose-ctl / .pose-scrub live-figure update lines and the .pose-photo-scrub input handler.

Re-emit the .pose3d / .pose-draw / .pose-photo container markup from the variation editor (this is the parked 'visual variation picker' rebuild — that markup never existed in the live build, so it must be authored).
npm run typecheck && npm test && npm run build.
